signals.reasoning: BTC is constructive, but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH has a mild bullish lean, but not enough to justify fresh risk while the event brake is active.
SOL is too mixed to justify action under the veto regime.
DASH remains one of the best long candidates on pure mechanics, but veto should keep it in watch mode rather than as a fresh buy.
HEMI is a clean bearish fade because funding is strongly positive, crowding is high, and the market still looks vulnerable to reversal.
GRIFFAIN lacks enough clean edge to justify action here.
FOGO remains the clearest bearish name on the board because both price and mechanics continue to lean decisively negative.
STRK has some constructive squeeze-long traits, but veto keeps it below the threshold for a fresh long.
CHILLGUY remains extended and crowded, so it stays watch-only under the veto regime.
ZEREBRO still lacks the trade quality and clear edge needed for action.
